r_code:
# Create a renamed copy of the S&P 500 data
spx_renamed <- spx_daily_5y %>%
  rename(
    SPX_Open   = Open,
    SPX_High   = High,
    SPX_Low    = Low,
    SPX_Close  = Close,
    SPX_Volume = Volume
  )

# Create a renamed copy of the Bitcoin data
bitcoin_renamed <- bitcoin_daily_5y %>%
  rename(
    BITCOIN_Open   = Open,
    BITCOIN_High   = High,
    BITCOIN_Low    = Low,
    BITCOIN_Close  = Close,
    BITCOIN_Volume_USD = Volume_USD,
    BITCOIN_Circulating_Supply_Est = Circulating_Supply_Est,
    BITCOIN_Market_Cap_Est = Market_Cap_Est
  )

# Merge the renamed S&P 500 and Bitcoin data on Date (inner join)
local({
  merged_data <- inner_join(spx_renamed, bitcoin_renamed, by = "Date")
  cat("Rows after merge (overlapping dates):", nrow(merged_data), "\n")
  # make the merged data available for the next steps
  assign("merged_data", merged_data, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  rm(merged_data)   # clean up the temporary object inside the local block
})

local({
  # Make sure Date is of class Date
  if (!inherits(merged_data$Date, "Date")) {
    merged_data$Date <- as.Date(merged_data$Date)
  }
  
  # Plot the returns
  p <- ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = Date, y = Return, colour = Asset)) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(
      title = "Daily Returns: S&P 500 vs Bitcoin",
      x = "Date",
      y = "Return"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  print(p)
  
  # Clean up the intermediate renamed tables
  rm(spx_renamed, bitcoin_renamed, envir = .GlobalEnv)
})

```r
      title = "30‑Day Returns: S&P 500 vs Bitcoin",

      y = "30‑Day Return"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  print(p)
})
```
